rating 1.0. What I got was a Steve Spangler Science Behind Magic kit with experiments that were pretty basic. I am pretty sure that this kit I had my doubts about trying this because there was no way of seeing what you were getting. Sure enough, we got something that we already had and done three years ago. I joined the 8-13 year old STEM Club in hopes of getting something truly educational and challenging. What I got was a Steve Spangler Science Behind Magic kit with experiments that were pretty basic. I am pretty sure that this kit was sub $15 dollars when we got it for our kids three years ago. They are definitely not enough to get my 9 year old excited enough to do them again.
I was hoping for something along the lines of a Tinker Crate subscription. Not getting to see what you are getting, for people that buy STEM stuff for their kids on a regular basis, is not worth it. I think I am just better off buying my kids a STEM activity once a month so I know what I am getting.

rating 5.0. Investigative learning disguised as fun! Only received 1 so far, but I am very pleased!  First one was a lazer light maze kit.  (There are multiple pieces to redirect the lazer with the objective to get the lazer to the end piece.  It came with a set of challenge cards to follow that start off super easy & get progressively more challenging.  My 8 year old son loves it & I have caught my 18 year old son playing with it several times!)  I figured I would try it for the introductory price &, if my son didn't like it, I would cancel.  I like that there are multiple options (1 month, 2 month, skip this shipment, etc.) and, because it is Amazon, I know I can return it if it isn't appropriate.  (I also plan to take them for my 5th graders to test out as well.)  Every child should be getting these kits.  It gets them off of their screen and actively engaged in the investigative process where they have the opportunity to learn by doing which is the most powerful education tool you can use!

rating 5.0. Best Masks I've Used! I became a big fan of sheet masks while living in Japan. I usually bought cheap ones, though, and they weren't much more effective than just moisturizer. I also have very sensitive skin and sometimes I'd get a few zits after certain ones.

I have been busy and stressed lately, so I decided to treat myself to something small. I tried FaceTory. I have now used 8 masks and I have loved them all. None have irritated my skin, though a few did start to tingle. There are at least 2 that I felt made such a difference I would buy them again separately. I also like that there is a little explanation of what each one is supposed to do. It's fun to try different masks instead of being locked into basically different scents of the same mask, which is how I feel about sets I've bought on Amazon.

It does come in an envelope, not a box, but I don't see that as an issue. I also haven't had any problem with the size of the masks as some reviews mention. I have a wide, Mexican face and I'm fine with them. Like most sheet masks, I do sometimes cut extra slits around the eye holes so they lay flatter against my face instead of being stretched across.

My 3rd envelop should be arriving soon and I don't see myself unsubscribing any time soon.

I have been using these masks once a week. Combined with the rest of my skincare, my skin has never been better and I haven't felt dry at all this winter.
rating 5.0. Live the variety and mix up every month Best subscribe and save on the market
rating 5.0. Facetory is amazing I love all the face mask

rating 5.0. Good Mask Selection, Good Price I have the 4-pack monthly subscription. It's the perfect amount for me. I look forward to it arriving in the mail every month in its cute envelope. There's always something interesting and different to discover. My skin has never looked better!

They include a card in each pack that explains what each mask does, along with some tips. This is very helpful because sometimes the mask packaging is not in English. I usually get a couple of extra uses out of the leftover mask goo...err...serum.

It doesn't arrive in a box. It arrives in a cute printed plastic envelop and is delivered with your regular mail. I think you have to subscribe to one of the higher levels to get the box. I don't mind the delivery method and none of my masks have arrived damaged.

At $8.90, it comes out to around $2.22 per mask. Masks in my local area are typically around $2.50 and they can be up to $5 per mask depending on the brand and the store. These are equal in quality and sometimes better than what I can find. So this is a very good value for me.

But the real savings comes from the way it stops me from buying random (sometimes expensive!) masks from whatever store I happen to be in. This is much easier on my budget and I love looking forward to each month's mask surprises.

I've had this subscription for about 6 months and I'm very happy so far. I expect to remain subscribed for a while.

Photos:
This is just what I have leftover lying around. Each delivery comes with 4 masks. I'm saving the gold one for something special. You can see the Tea Tree one is the FaceStory brand. It's a 2 step mask with an extra serum packet at the top. I've included some photos of the bag. It's some kind of foil-lined plastic. It holds everything nicely. Each face mask has ingredients that usually help my oily skin and large pores. Relaxing and taking 20 min to enjoy life with a face mask really helps!!! I wish I could continue my membership.

rating 5.0. Good Mask Selection, Good Price I have the 4-pack monthly subscription. It's the perfect amount for me. I look forward to it arriving in the mail every month in its cute envelope. There's always something interesting and different to discover. My skin has never looked better!

They include a card in each pack that explains what each mask does, along with some tips. This is very helpful because sometimes the mask packaging is not in English. I usually get a couple of extra uses out of the leftover mask goo...err...serum.

It doesn't arrive in a box. It arrives in a cute printed plastic envelop and is delivered with your regular mail. I think you have to subscribe to one of the higher levels to get the box. I don't mind the delivery method and none of my masks have arrived damaged.

At $8.90, it comes out to around $2.22 per mask. Masks in my local area are typically around $2.50 and they can be up to $5 per mask depending on the brand and the store. These are equal in quality and sometimes better than what I can find. So this is a very good value for me.

But the real savings comes from the way it stops me from buying random (sometimes expensive!) masks from whatever store I happen to be in. This is much easier on my budget and I love looking forward to each month's mask surprises.

I've had this subscription for about 6 months and I'm very happy so far. I expect to remain subscribed for a while.

Photos:
This is just what I have leftover lying around. Each delivery comes with 4 masks. I'm saving the gold one for something special. You can see the Tea Tree one is the FaceStory brand. It's a 2 step mask with an extra serum packet at the top. I've included some photos of the bag. It's some kind of foil-lined plastic. It holds everything nicely. I store my masks in it to keep them organized.

rating 5.0. Best Masks I've Used! I became a big fan of sheet masks while living in Japan. I usually bought cheap ones, though, and they weren't much more effective than just moisturizer. I also have very sensitive skin and sometimes I'd get a few zits after certain ones.

I have been busy and stressed lately, so I decided to treat myself to something small. I tried FaceTory. I have now used 8 masks and I have loved them all. None have irritated my skin, though a few did start to tingle. There are at least 2 that I felt made such a difference I would buy them again separately. I also like that there is a little explanation of what each one is supposed to do. It's fun to try different masks instead of being locked into basically different scents of the same mask, which is how I feel about sets I've bought on Amazon.

It does come in an envelope, not a box, but I don't see that as an issue. I also haven't had any problem with the size of the masks as some reviews mention. I have a wide, Mexican face and I'm fine with them. Like most sheet masks, I do sometimes cut extra slits around the eye holes so they lay flatter against my face instead of being stretched across.

My 3rd envelop should be arriving soon and I don't see myself unsubscribing any time soon.

I have been using these masks once a week. Combined with the rest of my skincare, my skin has never been better and I haven't felt dry at all this winter.

rating 5.0. Just what I was looking for! I signed my 7 year old up for this program and we received our first box today. It came with three different activities. The set sold on Amazon for $29. Each one took us about 30 minutes to complete which was perfect. My 3 year old was also able to participate even though he didn't understand the concepts.

The box included an airplane kit which had several activities explaining how planes fly (lift, etc). The second was a crystal growing kit and the third was panning for fake gold.

I've run into several parents who's kids are in schools that are heavily focused on STEM. I hope this will help expose my kids to the same concepts in a fun way. I may also sign my son up when he turns four. I think 3 is too young for the cost.

If you're on the fence, it's worth a try. You can cancel it at anytime.

rating 5.0. An Impressive Start I have received two packages to date from the STEM Club subscription for my 5-year old daughter and have been very impressed by the selections. This month's offering was a robotic mouse and maze set that teaches your child how to code on a basic level - coding is needed to program the mouse's movements.  I thought it was a very creative approach to introduce the coding concept and my daughter really enjoyed it.  So far we are very pleased and looking forward to the next package!

rating 4.0. Time will tell I signed up for the 5-7 year old plan since I have a 5 and 6 year old at home. I jumped at this once I saw it. I always had fun educational toys to tinker with growing up, mostly from Radio Shack back then.

Kind of bummed out over the first toy. It clearly says 8+ on the box. We were able to get some use out of it, but it was defiantly over their heads/attention span, (Snap Circuits Fundamentals); trying to explain electrical currents to a 5 year old is difficult. Nothing against the product, It is a pretty nice setup. What happened is I played with it for about an hour and let the boys do what they could and I answered any questions they had. They defiantly got some use out of it, I just had to guide them quite a bit. Not a total loss and it will be there for when they are older. Could have been avoided with an age appropriate toy though, especially the first toy out of the gate like that.

The value is there though. I feel I got my $20 worth.

Edit 9-25-17:
I bumped it from 3 stars to 4. Not much has changed, it is still hit and miss. Some shipments have been great, some have not (I'm looking at you magic school bus.) But, the value has been there throughout. We got some great building sets and some fun experiments out of this and I plan on keeping the subscription for a while. I think some of the best things about this subscription are the assortment of products I probobly wouldn't buy on my own, the monthly consistency, and the price; you tend to get very good deals on what is sent. Dislikes are sometimes you end up with a dud, and sometimes the shipment doesn't quite fit in the age range intended.

rating 1.0. Wrong age group and not a great deal. I feel bad for writing a review on something that is still being shipped, but I'm disturbed that Amazon is misrepresenting this program. I will change the review if necessary once I open and use the first kit:

I have signed up for the 5-7 age group. The toy being shipped says it's for 8+ The set looks simple enough but the colored instructions seem pretty complicated for a 6 YO. The product is: https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01KZQZHE2/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o01_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1

So I just looked up the product online. The product is selling for $28 right now on Amazon so at first you think you're getting a good deal at $20 a month, but I see this product was being sold on Nov 7, 2016 for $17 on Amazon. I'm having serious doubts about this program right now as it looks like I'm getting a product that is not in my son's age group and I'm not getting a particularly good deal on it. I'm thinking now it's better to pick and choose your own STEM toys to make sure you're getting the right age group and a good price.

Again, A little unfair to review on a product that is still in delivery, but I wanted to let people know now.

rating 5.0. Love the Age 3-4 STEM Subscription-Safe Toys-Get This! The age 3-4 STEM Toy Subscription is great!! All the toys are age appropriate and safe for the little ones. Nothing toxic, just fun. Actually, my ten year enjoys planning with the toys from this subscription age level as well. The first item he received was this neat engineering building set made with extra large pieces for little hands, called "Kids First Automobile Engineer Kit." Next we are getting the "Educational Insights Design & Drill Activity Center."

I tried all the other age group subscriptions and this one is the best. The other two contained items with toxic warnings. But the age 3-4 doesn't, thank goodness.

As a parent, I want my kids to be safe and be able to play with STEM toys without my constant supervision. I have to be able to cook, clean and keep house too...Life is busy.

rating 3.0. Unrealized Potential The entire concept is definitely a great idea, and my son is well aware each month he's going to get a new STEM toy. Just seeing his excitement around what's coming for him is enough to keep the subscription going for now. At this age, we just want to make sure he's developing a love for STEM.

As a former science teacher, the quality of the actual toys/sets/kits selected could be so much better. The very first one was fantastic - it had three small "science fair" like experiments we did together in our kitchen, and each one had a sticker-like badge to give him upon completion. We were all excited about building a sense of cumulative growth by putting his badge stickers in his journal each month to see how many cool things we'd do. But alas, that great setup was only with the first manufacturer, and since then it has been a mix of items from many different manufacturers. Some are amazing, like a coding mouse + maze game. Others are pretty disappointing, like a big experiment kit that required buying additional supplies for every experiment. I'm hoping the quality of the monthly STEM Club goods increases soon. I'm not sure how long my son's (or my) excitement will last.

rating 4.0. Like a box of chocolates, you never know what you're gonna git! We're 3 months into STEM Club so far, and so far its "pretty good". My daughter is 6 years old, and I'm doing my darndest to make her a geek like me. Here's the breakdown...

Month 1, we received a kit to create your own bounce balls, a gyroscope kit, and a make your own dinosaur fossil kit. All in all, the 3 items combined were worth about $20, so you got your value, but the items weren't the most exciting for my daughter. We played with the bounce balls and gyroscope for about 10 minutes and then forgot about them.
- Kit cost: approximately $20.
- Entertainment: approximately 10 minutes.
- Ranking: 2/5

Month 2, we received a science kit which was a lot of fun. It contained items to make water bubble, ways to test for acids and bases, some fun stuff. All in all the kit was fun, it was once and done however, but we'd definately buy the kit again in the future.
- Kit cost: approximately $20.
- Entertainment: 2 hours.
- Ranking: 4/5

Month 3, we received a STEM Programmable Mouse, this thing is VERY cool. You essentially program a mouse to find the cheese by pressing forward, backward and turn buttons on the mouse. The kit comes with cards that help you teach the child how many button presses you need, etc. We've unpacked this thing at least 5 times since we received it, and my daughter really enjoyed it every time.We'd buy this as a gift for other kids in the future as well.
- Kit Cost $40
- Entertainment: 10+ Hours
- Ranking 5/5

rating 3.0. Just received the second of the same kit in 4 months. Wazzup Amazon? I purchased the STEM Club for my grandson who is just turning 5. 5 is a problematic age ... just beginning reading readiness for most kids so they may have trouble with reading some of the STEM vocabulary. I am sure that it is difficult for Amazon to come up with items for this age, Pre-K. I have a bright but not precocious grandson, A lot of things which do not require reading seem to be too simple for him or, if reading is required, he has trouble with some of the vocabulary. I read the reviews for the first kit, the "Young Scientist Club The Magic School Bus Engineering Lab". Frankly the kit was disappointing because some things worked, some things didn't and the whole thing was "cheesy". Thin cardboard, poorly thought out sequences of "experiments" and the like. His mom suggested I save my money, or give him a $20 bill. I said we would give it a second chance and we are glad I/we did. The second unit was the "Learning Resources Code & Go Robot Mouse Activity Set" which was a (stored program) robotic mouse with a travel platform (durable), some instructions (which we read and figured out together), cards which were an aid to figuring out the steps for the program, barriers and pictures with which to build obstacle courses and a magnetic wedge of cheese. Eli mastered the entire thing in under a half hour. He knew the mouse was a robot and the objective was to find the cheese, but he got the grasp of programming the mouse without considering the steps as "programming", he was just telling the mouse what to do. He showed off his new skill to his his mom, dad and grandma when they each arrived. I was excited to watch him learn. It provided for great conversation/discussion. This one was a hit. We are all looking forward to the next kit.
The Gears! Gears! Gears kit we received in July was a hit, but we were all disappointed to receive the second Young Scientist Club The Magic School Bus Engineering Lab kit in 4 months. We hated it the first time, please don't send it again.

rating 1.0. Wrong age group and not a great deal. I feel bad for writing a review on something that is still being shipped, but I'm disturbed that Amazon is misrepresenting this program. I will change the review if necessary once I open and use the first kit:

I have signed up for the 5-7 age group. The toy being shipped says it's for 8+ The set looks simple enough but the colored instructions seem pretty complicated for a 6 YO. The product is: https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01KZQZHE2/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o01_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1

So I just looked up the product online. The product is selling for $28 right now on Amazon so at first you think you're getting a good deal at $20 a month, but I see this product was being sold on Nov 7, 2016 for $17 on Amazon. I'm having serious doubts about this program right now as it looks like I'm getting a product that is not in my son's age group and I'm not getting a particularly good deal on it. I'm thinking now it's better to pick and choose your own STEM toys to make sure you're getting the right age group and a good price.

Again, A little unfair to review on a product that is still in delivery, but I wanted to let people know now.

The idea for the club is great, first because doing group orders they can offer items that are more expensive just for the group fee, but second because they can curate the contents and expose us and our children to things we don't know and can help in their development. 1st Month: (**)

Amazon, please, we know you can do much more with this club, it is a great initiative if it is well executed, don't disappoint us!

rating 2.0. Wanted to love it!, but..... I wanted to love this subscription, I really did. The idea of it was amazing. The wonderful people at Amazon curating the plethora of STEM toys and picking the right ones to send people. Great in theory.

Execution... not so good.

1) the toys were "barely" over the price of the subscription. Not much of a "deal".

2) they did not come every month. they came infrequently. I think we ended up getting 3 kits in about 6 months...

3) no creativity or imagination in what they sent out. i was hoping for them curating beyond what i could do with a quick google search -- not so much. But, the straw that broke the camel's back: At the beginning of March, my son got the Kids First Automobile Engineer Kit -- it was great. After two long months, he got his next kit... the Kids First Aircraft Engineer Kit. Basically the same kit. Where is the imagination? Where are all the smart Amazon people coming up with creative options?

So, we cancelled. Oh well.

8 to 13 yr old kit. For our first month (March) we got a building set and learned all about levers, Axel's, wheels, pullys, etc... My 9 yr old loves it! He's already built 3 structures and plans in building them all.
April- We got a circuit kit. I think this is my boys favorite thus far..there are so many things you can do with it and he has already completed the entire book!
May- we got the Lego architect Eiffel Tower. The instruction book has information and history on the Eiffel Tower that we will be reading together tonight before bed.
June- Gravity maze. A logic game where you build the maze with blocks and get the marble to hit it's target. The cards tell you what your supposed to use and you figure out how to build it. GREAT logic game!!!

3/4 year old kit
April- Automobile engineering blocks. Came in a tote, colorful, big, and perfect for preschoolers! Very happy with this kit!
May-Design and drill kit. Son loves this, he has spent hours on it!! There is a strong chemical smell coming off the plastic though especially in the drill. I've been. Airing it out for a week now and the odor is still there though it is less potent.
June-Gears building set. This is so much fun!! I think all ages would have fun with this set!

rating 5.0. I have a 2 y/o and 4 y/o and they have loved all of these We have been subscribing for 5+ months so far with the 3-4 year old age range. I have a 2 y/o and 4 y/o and they have loved all of these. Even our friends kids who visit play with the different kits. I'm overall satisfied with the subscription and am going to change it to the 5-7 age range. below are short reviews on each kit we have received.
We got the gears kit and built some of the models. They showed a robot on the bucket however it wasn't in the instruction booklet so we had a blast trying to figure it out as a family. The platforms and gears are sometimes hard to take apart but other then that a good toy, plus it came in a nice hard plastic bucket for storage and additional play.
We received the Automobile Engineer kit and the 4 y/o and his friends love to build the different cars (the book has instructions for at least 4). They either don't want to take it apart or they like to crash it to bits (but that's what boys do). The kit had color coded pieces to indicate different lengths and sizes which is sometimes hard for the kids to differentiate and the instructions aren't written piece by piece. Overall it provides just enough learning with limited adult assistance.
We received the Cod-a-pillar recently and my kids don't play with it. We have limited hard flooring that the kids can play on so it doesn't have room to roam. they both already have remote controlled toys and cars so this is slow and boring waiting for it to turn. Although the most expensive kit so far it's the least enjoyable for our family.
We received the drill and screw peg board thing and it's so much fun for both kids. It comes with interchangeable bits for an electric and hand drill, different color screws and pattern maps. the little one learns dexterity and the use of tools and the older one follows the patterns. They actually play well together on this one and the older one teaches the little one the right pattern. Love this kit! (the drill goes clock wise and counter clock wise to remove screws).
We also received the Math Playbox. It's the most traditional kit since it teaches simple math. the pieces are hard pressed cardboard vice wood (they look like wood on the pic). It requires adult assistance to explain the math principles because it's built like a puzzle and my child just matches the pieces to the holes. It reinforces his numbers and allows him to visualize the math problems. It bores me as an adult but I don't get complaints when I suggest we play with it so I'll take it as a win.

rating 5.0. Stem club worth it LOVE LOVE LOVE Stem club. Toys better than expected. Both 5-8 and older option. Highly recommend.

rating 5.0. So worth it! I bought this for my 2 year old son. He is somewhat advanced for his age so I wasn't worried about the age group suggestions. I have the subscription sent to his grandparents house so when he visits them every month he has a new and cool surprise. The items that have come so far have been engaging, fun and child approved lol. All of the items we have received so far have also been of great quality and were definitely worth the price of the STEM club. Most of the items are very large, and have plenty of material to keep a preschooler busy for sometime. So far we have a wood math set, a bucket of construction gears, a car building set, a coding caterpillar, a science exploration kit, and a patterned drill set.

I will note that some of the items could potentially be difficult for a young person (2-3) to immediately grasp and may require more in depth adult assistance to navigate, but they're great all the same.

The shipping is also great, at the end of the month (for us) amazon notifies me of shipping and the package always arrives on time or early even.

Pros:
Price
Quality
Variety of toys
Ease of order
Packaging

Possible Cons:
Some toys may have a larger learning curve you really just have to know your child. The great thing with that is that they can learn to love certain toys as they age a little or even play with them with adult assistance.

I would definitely recommend purchasing this club subscription to anyone with little ones.

rating 5.0. Best investment for our homeschool family! Our 7 yr old son LOVES this subscription (along with our DS4)! He is very gifted in regards to STEM, so we selected the 8-13 age range. The items we have received so far are very durable and we look forward to saving them for our younger children to use in the future. We have incorporated these kits into our homeschooling, since they are often filled with educational information. The kits that aren't have been primarily logic based problem solving kits or something that we research further on our own.

When we originally decided to subscribe, we agreed to try it out for 3 months to see if it was worth it, knowing we could cancel at any time. We had a few issues with delivery and other technical support issues, all of which Amazon handled amazingly, compensating for our inconvenience! We started back in July and will continue our subscription till he needs something more challenging.

We highly recommend this subscription to everyone we know with kids. Best one out there!

rating 2.0. 8-13 is bad. Others great. I have had a great experience in the quality of the lower age toys; however, this first month adding 8-13 is extremely disappointing. This "mechanics" set is filled with quizzes and lessons but none of the projects are actually mechanically sound. Beyond that, every project we have tried has had a minimum of 3 errors in the diagram to the point where the instructions actually contradict themselves.

Finally, (and this was the last straw for me) the "catapult" used as an example of a lever was supposed to be used by flipping it with a finger. Catapults use tension (and levers) but even though it would have been idiotically simple to add a rubber band, these "STEM experts" were apparently happier to ignore the concept of tension and just flick the toy across the room. And let's be real: a catapult is a poor example in the first place considering a trebuchet far better illustrates the concept.

Obviously this toy is meant to capitalize on parents who like the idea of STEM toys but have no actual experience in the field of physics or engineering while filling your child's head with incomplete theories and bad science.

Ugh.

But the 3-8 is great :) we'll be keeping those two.

rating 5.0. So worth it! I bought this for my 2 year old son. He is somewhat advanced for his age so I wasn't worried about the age group suggestions. I have the subscription sent to his grandparents house so when he visits them every month he has a new and cool surprise. The items that have come so far have been engaging, fun and child approved lol. All of the items we have received so far have also been of great quality and were definitely worth the price of the STEM club. Most of the items are very large, and have plenty of material to keep a preschooler busy for sometime. So far we have a wood math set, a bucket of construction gears, a car building set, a coding caterpillar, a science exploration kit, and a patterned drill set.

I will note that some of the items could potentially be difficult for a young person (2-3) to immediately grasp and may require more in depth adult assistance to navigate, but they're great all the same.

The shipping is also great, at the end of the month (for us) amazon notifies me of shipping and the package always arrives on time or early even.

Pros:
Price
Quality
Variety of toys
Ease of order
Packaging

Possible Cons:
Some toys may have a larger learning curve you really just have to know your child. The great thing with that is that they can learn to love certain toys as they age a little or even play with them with adult assistance.

I would definitely recommend purchasing this club subscription to anyone with little ones.

rating 4.0. Usually keeps child entertained, but also have lots of parts to get lost. So far we enjoy the Stem club, although our son is 2 and a half. The first two packages we got (the automobile maker and drill set) have a LOT of parts. We liked the automobile maker because he could also use the parts to build other things. The drill and screwdriver board seems to have less practical applications. You are sort of just drilling screws into a board.

Both activities have kept him busy for decent periods of time, and are kits we probably wouldn't have bought on our own. Our son loves anything that involves taking something apart or building something, so we will probably stay subscribed for awhile.

rating 2.0. Wanted to love it!, but..... I wanted to love this subscription, I really did. The idea of it was amazing. The wonderful people at Amazon curating the plethora of STEM toys and picking the right ones to send people. Great in theory.

Execution... not so good.

1) the toys were "barely" over the price of the subscription. Not much of a "deal".

2) they did not come every month. they came infrequently. I think we ended up getting 3 kits in about 6 months...

3) no creativity or imagination in what they sent out. i was hoping for them curating beyond what i could do with a quick google search -- not so much. But, the straw that broke the camel's back: At the beginning of March, my son got the Kids First Automobile Engineer Kit -- it was great. After two long months, he got his next kit... the Kids First Aircraft Engineer Kit. Basically the same kit. Where is the imagination? Where are all the smart Amazon people coming up with creative options?

So, we cancelled. Oh well.

input: rating 4.0. Awesome Box - Wish there was a way to indicate customer considerations/preferences at sign up I love my Therabox, it was the “Pamper” box this past month and I loved all of the contents.  The only reason I did not give five stars is because while each box had 8 items they were not the same, three out of the five you got one of a few similar items.  For example on the Florapy face mask you either received “strength and energy” or “stress-relief” and there is no way to personalize/choose.  I received the strength and energy mask but honestly it has been really stressful as a nurse during this outbreak and I would have preferred the other mask.  Don’t get me wrong I will definitely continue my Therabox subscription at least for now but while I understand we aren’t “curating our own boxes” maybe a survey for customers about ourselves or our preferences would be nice to help make sure we are ultimately thrilled about each and every item in the box.

rating 1.0. Tasteless, useless, old products. Rude, uncaring customer service. AVOID. Got a box of mostly shredded paper. The contents were from an old, leftover box. A few of the things were nice, but over half were useless. I got a sheet of stickers - what adult needs such things? Also got a travel journal, during the middle of a pandemic. Not only is it useless since travel is both dangerous and outright banned at the moment, but it is most assuredly tasteless at a time when we have so many dead from a disease that spreads through travel. I got a box of old products mixed with junk that was mostly paper. When I contacted the company their reply was not only rude, but they basically told me oh well, not sorry, you should have expected old products when you bought a "mystery box" which is not how this item was listed. No offer to make things right. They didn't even acknowledge that the travel journal was in poor taste, just said to save it for after the pandemic. Not only do I NOT RECOMMEND this box, but I will be avoiding this company in the future. Yes the box was discounted, but even the lowered price wasn't a good value. If I had paid full price I would have been even more upset. Wish I could give ZERO STARS!

rating 5.0. Love this box subscription! I get practically every subscription box there is: glossybox, fitfabfun, singleswag, birchbox, Ipsy, etc etc. some of these I stopped though because I felt they were getting more expensive with less in the boxes. I saw therabox on amazon and wanted to try it out even though it’s a bit pricey and even though I’m not even really a health/wellness/natural/etc fanatic. I mean I like being healthy as best I can but I was worried it would be a bunch of like meditation/yoga/crystals/patchouli type of box. Nothing wrong with that but it’s not for me. Some of it is stuff I probably never would have seen or found in stores or even thought of looking for. So, I recommend this box fully. I think it’s my favorite one I get out of them all.

rating 5.0. Best self-care gift I could have given myself! I first purchased TheraBox at the beginning of the COVID-19 pandemic. I was having a hard time dealing with isolation, and was not taking good care of myself. I stumbled upon TheraBox when browsing Amazon, and it sounded like a great idea so that I could get some self-care goodies to take care of myself during an incredibly difficult time. I also am really bad at picking out my own skincare items, because I can be indecisive, so this was a good way to get different types of self-care products to try.

When I received my first box (pictured) I was shocked at how much I got for the price! The products are full-size and I love the variety of items included in the box - skincare, nails, hair, body, and more. This has allowed me to try all sorts of things I otherwise never would have tried, and I have added a bunch of these products into my daily routine. I also love that it comes with a fun activity each time to help boost my mood.

I thought that I would only get TheraBox for a few months, but 9 months later I am still getting my monthly TheraBox and loving it as a nice surprise for myself each month! I highly recommend TheraBox for anyone who wants to try more self-care products, or even better as a gift for a loved one.

rating 5.0. I love these!  I am especially happy they come every month, not quarterly. Every month a box arrives and makes me smile.  If there is something in it I can't use, I toss it in a drawer for use as a gift for someone else at another time.  I did that a lot last Christmas, and they were well-received.

rating 4.0. Awesome Box - Wish there was a way to indicate customer considerations/preferences at sign up I love my Therabox, it was the “Pamper” box this past month and I loved all of the contents.  The only reason I did not give five stars is because while each box had 8 items they were not the same, three out of the five you got one of a few similar items.  For example on the Florapy face mask you either received “strength and energy” or “stress-relief” and there is no way to personalize/choose.  I received the strength and energy mask but honestly it has been really stressful as a nurse during this outbreak and I would have preferred the other mask.  Don’t get me wrong I will definitely continue my Therabox subscription at least for now but while I understand we aren’t “curating our own boxes” maybe a survey for customers about ourselves or our preferences would be nice to help make sure we are ultimately thrilled about each and every item in the box.

rating 4.0. I think I am falling in love...... Updated Review:
So now on box 5, I have to say the items have been getting progressively much better!!! The October box was really an impressive assortment and I definitely received more then I paid for. The value was $170!! Facial cleansers, moisturizer,a mask, toner, etc.

Initial review: I've received 3 boxes to date, and I can't say I've been overly wow'd by what I've received for the price I pay. To put it in perspective, I spend $15 a month to receive the Allure box, and I feel I get significantly better value for what I pay. And most of the things are very applicable for use immediately. For this box, I have not once yet thought wow, that was really a great box and I made out like a bandit on it. A lot of the things I don't even use and end up giving them away or putting them to the side. I hope this changes because I love the concept. Just not the contents. So far.

rating 5.0. Fun, comforting items abound I was incredibly pleased with the therabox!  It was full of interesting products that I wouldn't have thought to purchase, but I was quite happy with all of them.  I was looking for a little pick my up treat for myself, and this definitely fit the bill.  I was so impressed that I am ordering a box for my good friend for her up coming birthday.

rating 2.0. Overpriced for Cheap Items $40 for THIS??? Seriously??? Who are they kidding? 3 of the items I can get at Target for around $15 and then get a $5 coupon back (a face mask, toner, face wipes- these items are so cheap!). A small candle- the $5 size, a “volcano” bracelet (which, I must admit, I was a bit excited about, at first, because I had looked into these in the past and had wanted one) that was so cheap it broke within a week, a lavender pillow spray/sheet mist, and some kind of roll on essential oil. Then there were 2 books both of which were a bit on the silly side. 1 was meant for a very specific audience- ones who travel around the world, frequently and like to journal their “adventures” and want to record all the clothes they pack for their trips (why?). The second one was a scratch off book. You’re supposed to scratch one bubble a day then you “have” to do what it says and it says ridiculous things- “light a candle”, “watch the sun rise”, “meditate”. Generic things that honestly you are either already doing or really have no interest in doing.
It’s a good idea “in theory” but it’s WAAAAAYYYYY overpriced!!! I may have kept the subscription going for $25/mo, but $40/mo is SUCH a ripoff. Plus, you end up with extra crap in your house that you absolutely don’t want or need.
I can say though, the packaging is lovely and it was fun to receive a “surprise” package in the mail. So...there’s that...

rating 5.0. Get rid of my winter blues!! This is my first time joining a monthly box subscription program. Therabox sounded like a great idea to help fight the winter blues, especially with my now, full time work from home life.

I have received one box and loved it. A couple of products I’m obsessed with and use daily. I’m excited to see what will be in future boxes. It’s nice to have something small to be excited about!! There was one item that I know won’t use, but I have a good friend that will, so it’s fun to share!

I did have one missing item, but have a replacement in transit now. I emailed CS and it was an easy process to request replacement. I also joined the FB group last night and I’m looking forward to the dialogue there as well.

Highly recommend this fun little investment in self care!

rating 1.0. Wet, everything was wet and nasty when the box arrived! What a nasty mess I was given to give as a gift! When the box arrived, it was dripping with a liquid... I contacted their customer service department and sent emails with pictures like they requested, but never got a response after sending the pictures. So I don't know if this is going to be corrected or not. For me, it was a big waste of money, but I guess my daughter got a okay few things out of the wet nasty box. Oh and I have no idea why, but one of the items in this box was a set of salt and pepper shakers that look like rocks. All the paperwork that talks about the products was destroyed and we are unable to read about what we received, so we guess that is what they are.

rating 4.0. I like it but don't love it This is my first time getting the box and I like it but definitely don't love it.  I got 8 items.  One item had to be replaced b/c the original company went out of business due to Covid.  The original product was supposed to be eye cream.  They replaced it with eye liner.  Huge disappointment.  The eye liner was about half the price the eye cream was and I was really excited to get the eye cream.  I wish they would have replaced it with something similar-some sort of face product if they couldn't do eye cream.  The salt and pepper set it meh at best.  Mine came broken so waiting to hear it they'll replace it (they said on the sheet they will).  Even so I might use that once.  There were wipe and face mask spray.  Both those are nice, but I never would have bought on my own.  There was also some facial/hair products that I do enjoy.  I liked and will use about 50% of the products, but I think that's to be excepted.  I am going to try one more month to see if I like the next box better.  Otherwise, I can't justify a monthly subscription if I'm not loving more of the products.  However, overall the concept is great.  I love the anticipation of getting something.  Feels like I'm treating myself.  I love that I'm basically buying myself a surprise gift every month.  Its fun to plan out how to use the products I do like.  I feels like a treat, which is what part of the point of the box is.  So we'll see if the next box is a little better.  Hoping it is so I can continue b/c I definitely want to like it!

input: rating 5.0. Gifted Bokksu - love it! This was given to me as a gift and I love it. The box is really nice and it came to me packed with snacks and tea. The book that it is sent with breaks down everything in the box and shares a lot of information. I also love that the box has a theme and it teaches you about Japan - the geography, language, etc. They also say this box is authentic and I believe it. It shipped directly to me from Japan. I shared the treats with others and we all sipped the tea and read the booklet - it was honestly a really fun experience and I look forward to doing it again.
rating 5.0. Delicious, savory, authentic Japanese snacks! I’ve already ordered a box as a gift and will continue this service. Beautifully presented, each individual package has its own character.

rating 5.0. The Best Gift My neighbor brought me a Bokksu as a gift, and I was pleasantly surprised.  The presentation was gorgeous, and it came with a well-designed pamphlet describing all the snacks and where they came from.  Everything I tried was interesting and delicious.  There was mochi, rice crackers, cookies, candy, chips, doughnuts, dried noodles, and even tea!  I really loved all the different flavors, and I could tell a lot of thought had been put into choosing each item.  I liked it so much, I bought two subscriptions.  One even came with special Kit Kat flavors like Apple Pie and Chestnut.  I'm going to look forward to getting my Bokksu every month and experiencing a box full of snacks and surprises.  It's great for families, Japanese snack lovers, foodies and it definitely makes the perfect gift.

rating 3.0. Sweets were great, savory sort of gross Too much savory in this one. I got it on a goldbox deal for $24ish, not worth the full price. Very beautifully presented but just not enough "wow" here to keep up subscription.

rating 5.0. A good selection of Japanese treats I received my box a week earlier than expected which was a nice surprise. The contents were well packed and arrived intact. There was a good selection of snacks all around the theme of Valentine's day. The amount of snacks and tea was more than I was expecting which made it feel like the price was fair for what I got. I'm looking forward to the next month's box and what treats it will have in it. The box does require a signature on delivery which is something I didn't expect, I may have missed that in the description somewhere. Overall the selection of snacks was good and the insert that told where they were from in Japan and a little about them was a great touch.

rating 2.0. It arrived quickly, but... I usually don’t like leaving bad reviews, but this was a bit irritating. I got this box for a gift to a friend since he’s really into the Japanese culture. rating 5.0. Loved it! Shipped from Japan, was certainly a treat for me and my girlfriend!

rating 4.0. Loved the geographic theme First box was winter goodies from Hokkaido. The box is a keeper. The presentation was very nice. The snacks were unique and I wished there had been more. However, the uniqueness and presentation make up for any other shortcomings. Very happy with this item.

rating 4.0. Good savory snack box I've subscribed to TokyoTreat in the past, but cancelled before a move. I decided to try Bokksu when Amazon had a deal on it. I've only gotten one box so far and I like it. I'll keep it for a little while longer even though the discount ended at the first box.

The big difference between this and TokyoTreat is that TokyoTreat has a bottled drink or two and has more mainstream sweet snacks. Bokksu seems to be dry teas and more savory snacks than sweets. It's also less mainstream stuff (ie no Pocky). They are both different so it depends on your tastes as to which is better.

Of course, what you get in the box is a lot less than what you'd get for the same money if you could buy it yourself, but that's true with any of these Japanese subscription services - you pay for the handling and especially shipping from Japan.

rating 1.0. Overpriced junk food The title says it all. An assortment of junk food and way over priced. If you have an Asian supermarket nearby, you can probably get 10 times the stuff and with better tasting snacks . I cancelled as soon as I see what is inside. It is shipped from Japan directly through mail, so I guess we are probably paying for the postage more than anything else.

rating 5.0. A very good  Japanese snackbox Over the years, I have subscribed to several Japanese snack boxes and Bokksu is one of the best. The snacks are not the cheap daigashi (cheap candy) that many of the other boxes include. The snacks included in this subscription  seem to be higher quality and very diverse. I like that they include allergen and nutrition information, because I don't like fish or shrimp, so I could easily avoid fishy flavored snacks. They also state if the snack is vegetarian, even in they classic box, so it is good for families that have member with eating restrictions such as vegetarian or halal.  The themes are cute and the inclusion of tea is a nice touch. I did contact customer support and the answered my question the same day.  I highly recommend.

rating 2.0. Interesting for the exploration aspect, but not really worth it for the price Got the August 2019 box when it went on sale for something like 40% off. Just received the box yesterday, and we haven't had the time to go through everything yet. Of the 5 or so things we've tried, 2 were good, 1 was interesting, and 2 were meh. I expect the rest of the items to have a similar mix of results, and I'll be sure to mark down the "good" ones and try to find them at my local Japanese grocer.

Even if every item were good, I think the regular box price would be on the 100% not worth it side of the scale. At 20-25 items per box, with some of those items being tiny, it comes to $1.56 to $1.95 per item. I typically expect more than your money's worth for mystery type boxes like this, and it's hard to see that being the case here. Needless to say, I won't be continuing my subscription.
rating 5.0. Wide variety of Japanese snacks We enjoyed the wide variety of snacks and the info provided with them. Looking forward to the next one!
rating 5.0. Great variety of snacks I've never seen before. I liked all the variety provided in the box.  It was very exciting.  I look forward to receiving another.

rating 5.0. They must put crack into this Ok, so I got the first box here half price with the rare Black Friday sale.  I figured I’d get one box then cancel before the full price subscription starts (make sure you do this as soon as it ships if you plan that).  Well, I did that, then got the box... finished it in 3 days and haven’t stopped thinking about it since!  Like any uncustomizable service, you might find some percentage of the set not to your tastes.  However, if you are a true connoisseur of Japanese snacks,  I think you will be impressed by the quality and variety of the offerings.  No, this is not cheap, although I did think it was definitely worth it for the sale price... full price, it is rather extravagant.  On the other hand, I don’t think you would find some of these unusual snacks anywhere else… Not at most nearby asian grocery stores (I live near 5, including large supermarket types with many Japanese snacks).  A few of these can certainly be classed “artisan” and not that mass-produced.  For example, the white chocolate infused strawberry.  I still cannot figure out how they made that.  It’s like the strawberry was actually injected with chocolate as it was freeze-dried, perfectly melded together.  That big spicy cracker thing and the pear cream biscuit were amazing, too.  Many of the other savory snacks also had a lot of complexity and depth to their flavors.  They deftly combine subtle sweet, sour, spicy, salty, and umami notes together.  They put American snacks to shame because these seem to be very one-note in comparison—just sickly sweet or too salty.  I think it’s cool how Bokksu includes a booklet so you can learn more about the snacks and Japanese culture, too (every box has a theme).  To be sure, these are ‘high end’ fancy snacks not for bargain shoppers or those with unadventurous and unelevated palates.  Look, I have easy access to Asian supermarkets within 5 minutes from me and I’m Chinese American—I’m well familiar with shrimp crackers, dried seaweed rice crackers, Pocky, mochi, etc; in contrast, these boxes are not run-of-the mill and is not for everyone.  You have to be willing to try different flavors and clearly, some of the reviewers who didn’t like it weren’t.  Anyway, after I finished the box, I couldn’t stop thinking about it.  After trying to ignore the cravings for a week, I ended up going on the website and purchasing a 6 month subscription for $230.  I never would have thought I’d pay that much for monthly snack boxes before,  but it is officially an addiction now! I’m sure it’s a luxury that I’m not going to be able to afford eventually… however,  I’m going to just try it out for a few months.  I don’t know if I can say $40 a box is “worthwhile” to most people, but for me, I must feed the obsession!
rating 5.0. Perfect for discovering new tasty Japanese snacks! Nice varieties, delicious sweet and salty snacks, and tea.

rating 5.0. Very cool selection, but not good for folks with celiac Our bokksu was a neat collection of snacks that were a fun experiment. Some tasted good, even though they also tasted strange and unfamiliar. Others were just plain delicious. The Bokksu came from Japan fresh and in perfect condition and the child unit we did this for was thrilled. We lucked out and got a box (July 2019) where only four items had wheat, which we promptly handed to Dad. The rest was safe to eat. Unfortunately, looking forward one month and back one month, those boxes had almost all products with wheat and only a couple that didn’t have wheat. Since we have celiac, that makes this interesting cultural exploration not as useful. Guess we’ll make the rounds of the local Asian markets and see if we can make our own collections. Too bad, this is a fantastic idea. It’s also cool that they have a vegetarian option. I’d recommend this for those without an allergy to wheat. That said, another very useful feature is the booklet, which lists the common allergens in each snack.

rating 5.0. Very interesting and different tastes than American snacks/candies It is amazing and fascinating to taste so many things with subtly different flavor profiles than anything you can buy in the US. Personally I feel that most of the snacks taste great. Some of the trends I have noticed are that the Japanese really like Strawberries especially with anything chocolate. Also many snacks are savory with lots of flavor (unami) without being super hot. The packaging is first rate and each shipment comes with a full color booklet explaining the origin of each snack and even has a map with what part of Japan it comes from. This is not for people who don't like to try new things, but it is wonderful for people who do.

rating 5.0. Nice selection Good variety with a well-written sheet explaining the contents.

rating 5.0. Awesome curated snacks! I absolutely love this! I got a promotion for a deal on the first box, but am definitely keeping my subscription based on my experience so far.

As others have mentioned, it is shipped from Japan so takes some time to arrive. It's a small box but it's packed - if you take everything out you have to be strategic putting it back or the lid won't close!

The biggest value here for me is the curation and quality of the snacks. A booklet is included with a profile and some details on each of the snacks, with a cool map of where they came from.

My favorite snack so far was the white strawberry, which according to the booklet is a white chocolate infused freeze dried strawberry that was made as an exclusive for Bokksu! I am far from being a fan of any freeze dried fruit but these were AMAZING, I want more!

All in all, while a little pricy, I totally think the experience and product I get out of it is worth it!

rating 5.0. Tasty and fun! I had just four of the snacks and I was hooked. I loved that I was given info about all of them and where they are from in Japan! Along with that I got a very friendly welcome as a new member! Thank you! I look forward to the next box.

rating 4.0. Cheaper than a trip to Japan A really nice presentation of a selection of snacks from Japan. I like the booklet that comes with it, explaining the origins of the foods, plus whether it's sweet or savory, contains alcohol, etc. It really ups the enjoyment level learning a little bit about the foods and the country at the same time.

My only complaint is that I wanted to specifically skip the August box since it had some strawberry sweets in it, and I despite that flavor. So, I waited until the Bokksu site said that September was shipping, and subscribed. So, naturally, August showed up at my doorstep anyway. Oh well. I hate that some of the goodies are going to be uneaten, but everything else inside was more my style, so it wasn't a total loss.

Despite shipping direct from Japan, it arrived right when they said it would, and in fine condition.

rating 5.0. High quality selection of snacks! I am impressed with the selection of snacks that come in this box. I even gifted it to a Japanese and they were impressed as well. See uploaded pics for an example of the included information booklet. It is a high quality pamphlet.

I do think it’s a little expensive for the amount of snacks you get. I would consider these higher end snacks, which almost justify the cost.

I do recommend Bokkusu. Tasty treats inside.

*** UPDATE *** I upgraded from 4 to 5 stars because I have been very satisfied with the quality of the snacks lately. Especially the white chocolate strawberry! It’s a freeze dried strawberry that they inject white chocolate into. It’s so creamy and tastes like strawberry cake. It’s just so unique, interesting, and delicious!

rating 1.0. Good Gift Idea, Not worth the money Great packaging and a great gifting idea, but the snack that it comes in are a joke. You can buy most if not all these candy at a local Asian market. If you live on the east coast or west coast, this would not be a problem. Let me warn you that the 20-25 piece of candy is extremely misleading. A tiny single wrap chocolate counts as one of those "piece" of candy. You are really getting 10-15 pieces of regular size Japanese candy and for Japanese candy, regular size is small, and small is one piece. If your looking for value, look else were, but if your looking to gift for around $20, this may be just about right.
rating 5.0. Great box Love it. Everything was always in order and made me wish I could afford it every month. I trested myself for two months and then canceled.

rating 3.0. Interesting taste of Japan.  Culture guide makes it more interesting too. My daughter in law lived in Japan during her senior year of high school.  I sent a box to my son and family to give them an idea of what it must have been like to live in Japan as she did.  The kids (aged from9-15)  had variable reviews regarding the taste of the foods.  But the culture guide was interesting to read.  I will probably cancel the subscription but it was an interesting dive into Japanese food culture.

rating 2.0. Nicely packaged. Questionable value. This is a box of individually packaged candies and snacks, sent directly from Japan. The treats come in a nice box, with a booklet explaining how each item fits into the month's theme. There are several items that are unique, such as hand made hard candy. But while the treats were tasty overall, many of them are sold in larger and cheaper packages at major Asian grocery stores in the US.  The treat box comes in a plain outer box, with a custom form declaring the package is worth the equivalent of about $20. This suggests shipping costs make up the difference. This may be worth it, if you don't have access  to an alternative. However, it seems excessive to order snack-sized versions of items I can buy at any time and at about half the price.

rating 5.0. Good Variety of Japanese Goodies The geographically/ thematic representative set of food selections is really nice, opened me up to a few things which I haven't even tried before in Japan, although it is slightly costly, it's totally worth it.

rating 5.0. CLASSY, TASTY AND FUN BUT NOT INEXPENSIVE I waited until I received one year's worth (four) Classic Bokksus to write my review.  First, regarding the quality; the boxes, accompanying literature, and the wrappers of each individual item in the boxes are striking.  The boxes themselves are refined and as solid as paper boxes can be.  I never received a damaged box or box with damaged contents.  Not a single item ever arrived stale.  I receive an email from Japan every few months notifying me that someone has mailed something to my address.  I'm not 100% sure but these might be the Bokksus because they seem to arrive shortly thereafter.  Everyone who saw the booklets commented on how beautiful the photographs were.  As to the contents, there's a good variety of sweet and savory in each box.  I like the little cakes and pastries the most, followed by the different types of tea.  I'm not as enamored with the hard candies that often leaves me feeling that I'm sucking on a sugar ball colored with artificial dyes.  I guess I expected more punch with natural flavors.  As several others have noted, the cost is rather high for a box full of snack items one might find on the impulse buying racks at a checkout lane at a convenience store.  Having lived in Japan before, I can say that while they taste good, some of the items, especially the salty snacks are pretty inexpensive to purchase there.  Taking into account the cost of shipping from Japan and the need for profit margin however, I suppose this is understandable.  I find myself having a hard time keeping up with the arrival of the next box.  That is, a new one arrives before I've finished the previous one.  This would make an outstanding gift subscription for someone interested in Japanese food/culture.

current_product_reviews: rating 4.0. Super fun and a nice selection, but expensive and mysterious I enjoyed this, but I Think it would be a lot better if they labeled things 'savory' and 'sweet.'  I like trying new things, but there are times I want savory, and not sweet or vice-versa.  But this is fun, although quite pricey.
other_product_reviews: rating 2.0. Junior box was disappointing I was disappointed with the junior box.  I may try again when he's old enough for the regular box.  Junior box didn't really seem to be well through through. The theme was 'on the farm' but there were no seeds, nothing about life cycles (should we tell kids where meat comes from--nothing here to guide that conversation).  There were 1) stickers to put on a picture of a barn; 2) a red gift box that they called a barn and suggested you save some stickers for it too; 3) noses to glue to Popsicle sticks; 4) pieces of felt to practice lacing together and 5) felt carrots with holes and tops with numbers to match the number of holes to the Arabic numeral.  There was also a bonus wooden frame that a kid could paint (the nicest part of the box).  Finally, there was an insidious sticker chart, with a 'badge' to encourage the parent to not cancel the box.  The included guide just suggested questions like why are farms important?  It didn't seem to me there was any science in it, ad the quality felt disappointing.  I think it is hard to do a box like this for pre-schoolers; it looks like the grade school box is of higher quality, so maybe we'll try that.

rating 1.0. Seriously disappointing mish-mash of stuff few would want This didn't feel like folks trying to get us to like new products. It felt like folks giving stuff they had too much of. I got
*1 lip masque
*Stick deodorant for my feet
* Blotting papers
* Aloe vera gel
*Cooling spray
*Black eyeliner.

I doubt I will use any of it.

input:
rating 2.0. Interesting for the exploration aspect, but not really worth it for the price Got the August 2019 box when it went on sale for something like 40% off. Just received the box yesterday, and we haven't had the time to go through everything yet. Of the 5 or so things we've tried, 2 were good, 1 was interesting, and 2 were meh. I expect the rest of the items to have a similar mix of results, and I'll be sure to mark down the "good" ones and try to find them at my local Japanese grocer.

Even if every item were good, I think the regular box price would be on the 100% not worth it side of the scale. At 20-25 items per box, with some of those items being tiny, it comes to $1.56 to $1.95 per item. I typically expect more than your money's worth for mystery type boxes like this, and it's hard to see that being the case here. Needless to say, I won't be continuing my subscription.

rating 3.0. Update: Price keeps going up! (My kids really enjoyed it) Update: At $35 dollars is was a pricey pleasure, then it jumped to $40 and now $45. I think it has finally priced me out.
Original review: This box was a decent value with decent quality treats :) I think it was great! I guess four stars is just because it didn't knock my socks off or anything, but if you are into sampling mid quality Japanese snacks (which my kids are) I really recommend giving this a try :)
rating 5.0. Amazing Wow. I ordered this on a whim, and it's totally worth it. The presentation is gorgeous and the guide tells you what each item is. There is no guessing. The box shipped fast!!!

rating 4.0. Loved the geographic theme First box was winter goodies from Hokkaido. The box is a keeper. The presentation was very nice. The snacks were unique and I wished there had been more. However, the uniqueness and presentation make up for any other shortcomings. Very happy with this item.
rating 5.0. interesting! my fiancé loves Japanese things! i got this box for his birthday and he loved everything about the box!

rating 1.0. Save your money. Food not good Horrible and do not recommend.  Waited over 6 weeks to get.  The items were not good at all and ended up being disgusting.  I wouldn’t ever buy again. Waste of money.
rating 4.0. Nice to try It was interesting to try but not worth the money
rating 5.0. Love it! Loved the variety of items! I really liked how it had a mix of sweet and savory snacks! The booklet was extremely helpful and put together really well!
rating 4.0. Neat but seriously overpriced Because of the pandemic the shipping for the April box was really bad, 90% of the items in the box have expired for months by the time the box got to me

rating 5.0. A little bit of Japan in a box! I really appreciated the packaging, which is typical for Japanese culture. The box seemed very high quality and the goods inside were packaged so neatly with guides as to where each item came from and what exactly to was in English. Many of the products were very delicious and it was a very nice variety. Perhaps if it was just a smidge cheaper, I could afford this on a monthly basis. All in all, it was a very good experience and can absolutely make a wonderful gift!

rating 3.0. Interesting overall I purchased boxes for three months.  In each box there is a mix of savory and sweet.  Crunchy and soft.  Some of treats are absolutely amazing and others not so much but that probably comes down to personal taste.  The items were packed well with very little damage to the treats.  It just seems the crunchy tends to be very spicy and or strongly flavored.  It is just a bit pricey for everything.

rating 5.0. Good Variety of Japanese Goodies The geographically/ thematic representative set of food selections is really nice, opened me up to a few things which I haven't even tried before in Japan, although it is slightly costly, it's totally worth it.
rating 5.0. Perfect for discovering new tasty Japanese snacks! Nice varieties, delicious sweet and salty snacks, and tea.
rating 4.0. Intro to Japanese foods, wish it had more gluten free options Most of my family cannot have gluten, so we had to check each package. Good news - alergy info is well done and clear.

rating 5.0. This box is the best so far! We have tried a bunch of different snack boxes from several countries and my son likes this ones the best so far. This is the only subscription he wants to keep. He said that there were some cookies with nuts in them in the last box that he particularly liked. He's looking forward to the next Bokksu. I'm excited because it makes him happy. He made it sound so fun that I ordered a box for myself, too.

Got my box that I shared with my little ones and they surprisingly loved almost everything in the box. My favorite was the Funwari Meijin Mochi Puffs that were Amazake flavored. They are so delicate and airy. Yum!!! Really enjoyed one I did not expect to called Fried Kakinotane which I thought tasted like spicy prawn crackers with horseradish mayo. I loved them! It says the actual flavor is called mentaiko Also loved the curry cashew nuts, the Fujiya Nectar Peach Mochi, the Yogurt Puru Mochi, the Mikan Mochi with bits of peel in it. The coconut sable cookies are good, too. Oh so was the One-bite Wagashi Matcha Warabi Mochi although I had to eat it in several bites because it's too much for one bite really. So fun!!! My kids liked everything. They really liked the Kinako dango and all of the mochi and the Senbei.

rating 5.0. Perfect gift for someone interested in Japanese culture I didn't realize this was shipped directly from Japan until I received it! Lots of very cool snacks and it includes a card with the description of everything. Every box has its own theme. The one I got was the Sakura theme, which wasn't my favorite as far as flavors go, but still very cool

rating 4.0. Nice but expensive and repetitive I really liked the first two or three boxes. There are a few artisenal items here and there but I wish there were more. I am not a very big fan of the hyper salty Japanese snacks and rice crackers can get boring too. So a box with just sweet items would be cool. There are too many convenience store industrial snacks for my taste and often you  receive multiple of the same item. I think the premise of seasonal and regional delicies is great but in the end there are too many similar snacks of somewhat dubious quality over the last few boxes. So I will cancel my subscription.

rating 5.0. Gifted Bokksu - love it! This was given to me as a gift and I love it. The box is really nice and it came to me packed with snacks and tea. The book that it is sent with breaks down everything in the box and shares a lot of information. I also love that the box has a theme and it teaches you about Japan - the geography, language, etc. They also say this box is authentic and I believe it. It shipped directly to me from Japan. I shared the treats with others and we all sipped the tea and read the booklet - it was honestly a really fun experience and I look forward to doing it again.

rating 5.0. A good selection of Japanese treats I received my box a week earlier than expected which was a nice surprise. The contents were well packed and arrived intact. There was a good selection of snacks all around the theme of Valentine's day. The amount of snacks and tea was more than I was expecting which made it feel like the price was fair for what I got. I'm looking forward to the next month's box and what treats it will have in it. The box does require a signature on delivery which is something I didn't expect, I may have missed that in the description somewhere. Overall the selection of snacks was good and the insert that told where they were from in Japan and a little about them was a great touch.

rating 5.0. High quality selection of snacks! I am impressed with the selection of snacks that come in this box. I even gifted it to a Japanese and they were impressed as well. It also comes with a little information booklet that give information about each snack which is unique. I don’t think you really get that anywhere else. See uploaded pics for an example of the included information booklet. It is a high quality pamphlet.

I do think it’s a little expensive for the amount of snacks you get. I would consider these higher end snacks, which almost justify the cost.

I do recommend Bokkusu. Tasty treats inside.

*** UPDATE *** I upgraded from 4 to 5 stars because I have been very satisfied with the quality of the snacks lately. Especially the white chocolate strawberry! It’s a freeze dried strawberry that they inject white chocolate into. It’s so creamy and tastes like strawberry cake. It’s just so unique, interesting, and delicious!
rating 5.0. Great variety of snacks I've never seen before. I liked all the variety provided in the box.  It was very exciting.  I look forward to receiving another.
rating 1.0. Very Disappointed ! Doesn't worth the money ! Just overpriced snacks which you can get from any Asian market at much cheaper prices .
rating 5.0. Great product Tastes great ,like both the snacks and information booklet

rating 4.0. Good product, has fish ingredients The box is a good value with great snacks. My only issue was matching  the snacks to the book pictures to see which had fish ingredients. None of the snacks had English text. The book was detailed and listed any ingredients one should know about, but I still found it incomplete. It is time consuming and not worth the end result.

rating 5.0. So much delicious treats! So I’ve been subscribed to the Bokksu for three months now, and I wanted to wait for a while before I could review. Now that I’ve sampled their three boxes, I can review!
What I love about Bokksu is that they comes in variety of sweet and savory treats straight from Japan, and they’re never same! I always appreciated their little book about the description of each treat.

However, the Bokksu isn’t for someone with nuts and seafood allergy as some savory treats will have shrimp or nuts.

Meaningless to say, I’m looking forward to the next box. So far, the Yuzu theme has to be my favorite!
rating 5.0. Delicious, savory, authentic Japanese snacks! I’ve already ordered a box as a gift and will continue this service. Beautifully presented, each individual package has its own character.
rating 5.0. Perfect little care package from Japan Very nicely packed and a good variety.

rating 5.0. The Best Gift My neighbor brought me a Bokksu as a gift, and I was pleasantly surprised.  The presentation was gorgeous, and it came with a well-designed pamphlet describing all the snacks and where they came from.  Everything I tried was interesting and delicious.  There was mochi, rice crackers, cookies, candy, chips, doughnuts, dried noodles, and even tea!  I really loved all the different flavors, and I could tell a lot of thought had been put into choosing each item.  I liked it so much, I bought two subscriptions.  One even came with special Kit Kat flavors like Apple Pie and Chestnut.  I'm going to look forward to getting my Bokksu every month and experiencing a box full of snacks and surprises.  It's great for families, Japanese snack lovers, foodies and it definitely makes the perfect gift.

rating 5.0. They must put crack into this Ok, so I got the first box here half price with the rare Black Friday sale.  I figured I’d get one box then cancel before the full price subscription starts (make sure you do this as soon as it ships if you plan that).  Well, I did that, then got the box... finished it in 3 days and haven’t stopped thinking about it since!  Like any uncustomizable service, you might find some percentage of the set not to your tastes.  However, if you are a true connoisseur of Japanese snacks,  I think you will be impressed by the quality and variety of the offerings.  No, this is not cheap, although I did think it was definitely worth it for the sale price... full price, it is rather extravagant.  On the other hand, I don’t think you would find some of these unusual snacks anywhere else… Not at most nearby asian grocery stores (I live near 5, including large supermarket types with many Japanese snacks).  A few of these can certainly be classed “artisan” and not that mass-produced.  For example, the white chocolate infused strawberry.  I still cannot figure out how they made that.  It’s like the strawberry was actually injected with chocolate as it was freeze-dried, perfectly melded together.  That big spicy cracker thing and the pear cream biscuit were amazing, too.  Many of the other savory snacks also had a lot of complexity and depth to their flavors.  They deftly combine subtle sweet, sour, spicy, salty, and umami notes together.  They put American snacks to shame because these seem to be very one-note in comparison—just sickly sweet or too salty.  I think it’s cool how Bokksu includes a booklet so you can learn more about the snacks and Japanese culture, too (every box has a theme).  To be sure, these are ‘high end’ fancy snacks not for bargain shoppers or those with unadventurous and unelevated palates.  Look, I have easy access to Asian supermarkets within 5 minutes from me and I’m Chinese American—I’m well familiar with shrimp crackers, dried seaweed rice crackers, Pocky, mochi, etc; in contrast, these boxes are not run-of-the mill and is not for everyone.  You have to be willing to try different flavors and clearly, some of the reviewers who didn’t like it weren’t.  Anyway, after I finished the box, I couldn’t stop thinking about it.  After trying to ignore the cravings for a week, I ended up going on the website and purchasing a 6 month subscription for $230.  I never would have thought I’d pay that much for monthly snack boxes before,  but it is officially an addiction now! I’m sure it’s a luxury that I’m not going to be able to afford eventually… however,  I’m going to just try it out for a few months.  I don’t know if I can say $40 a box is “worthwhile” to most people, but for me, I must feed the obsession!

rating 5.0. Very cool selection, but not good for folks with celiac Our bokksu was a neat collection of snacks that were a fun experiment. Some tasted good, even though they also tasted strange and unfamiliar. Others were just plain delicious. The Bokksu came from Japan fresh and in perfect condition and the child unit we did this for was thrilled. We lucked out and got a box (July 2019) where only four items had wheat, which we promptly handed to Dad. The rest was safe to eat. Unfortunately, looking forward one month and back one month, those boxes had almost all products with wheat and only a couple that didn’t have wheat. Since we have celiac, that makes this interesting cultural exploration not as useful. Guess we’ll make the rounds of the local Asian markets and see if we can make our own collections. Too bad, this is a fantastic idea. It’s also cool that they have a vegetarian option. I’d recommend this for those without an allergy to wheat. That said, another very useful feature is the booklet, which lists the common allergens in each snack.
